title: The Sick Village
openLocation: A still-functioning village south of Millhaven.
transport: Southbound on Thalassian horses; on foot inside the village.
immediateObjective: Assess an ordinary-looking outbreak site before it quietly becomes another Millhaven.
constraints: No grain confirmation before Jaina earns it from patient patterning; no cult proof before the granary scene.
No trust or softness overstatement — irritation stays even as Jaina stops treating Sylvanas as escort muscle.

povKnowsAtOpen: The survivor girl's testimony plus the follow-up tracking pass confirmed Millhaven's dead marched north toward Quel'Thalas.
Millhaven appears to be the first village on this road fully claimed; the country beyond still looks ordinary enough to hide milder outbreaks.
Her sensitivity lets the party notice trouble earlier than anyone else would, but not early enough to save a village once the grain is already moving.
The contamination is physical, pervasive, and lethal.
The magical signature is necromantic in the broad Kirin Tor sense, even if the banned applied theory behind it sits outside her education.
One survivor girl was found in Millhaven and sent north under Neral's escort on horseback.
Neral has already departed north and is no longer with the riding party.
The old seller and the girl's testimony together produced one weak clue about a newer grain seller, but nothing conclusive.
povDoesNotKnowAtOpen: How it first gets into people.
Whether this village can still be saved.
Whether someone is steering the outbreak or it is spreading on its own.
The exact point where sickness becomes the dead getting back up.
mustNotBeImpliedYet: No grain confirmation before Jaina earns it from patient patterning.
No cult proof before the granary scene.
No retroactive certainty about the old seller.
No suggestion that Jaina is ignorant of necromancy in the broad sense; her gap is in banned practice, not in knowing the subject exists.
castKnowsLeavingChapter: Grain is confirmed as what carries the plague into people, and the road and storehouses are how it spreads.
A live village watcher existed inside the sickness pattern and died by poison before he could be questioned.
Andorhal is now the next route objective.
The old seller / newer grain-man anomalies remain weak scraps in the file rather than proof.

pov.currentStanceAtOpen: If Jaina can name how the sickness is getting into people quickly enough, she can still keep this village from becoming another Millhaven.
pov.surfaceObjective: Find the entry path before the living village tips over into the same silence and bodies.
pov.pressureSource: The contamination is buried inside ordinary routines, and Sylvanas keeps seeing the human tells Jaina keeps treating as background.
pov.misbeliefManifestation: Jaina keeps reaching for the magical answer first and treating Sylvanas's people-read as supporting evidence instead of part of the core method.
pov.chapterTest: Every lead that looks purely arcane opens back into food, labor, timing, and village habit.
pov.forcedChoice: Keep driving toward a neat magical answer, or let Sylvanas's read of fear, routine, and supply reshape where Jaina looks.
pov.endStateShift: Jaina leaves with more evidence and less control. She still wants the clean mechanism, but she stops pretending Sylvanas is just escort muscle.
pov.carryForwardResidue: Chapter 5 opens with Jaina quicker to bring Sylvanas the messy human details instead of filing them as background noise.

throughPov.visibleFunction: The woman who can read fear, routine, and supply faster than Jaina can.
throughPov.povMisreadAtOpen: A sharp soldier, but still someone working below the level of the real answer.
throughPov.correctionEarnedHere: Sylvanas keeps landing on the pressure points Jaina misses, which forces Jaina to admit this is not lesser intelligence, just a different cut through the same problem.
throughPov.interactionRule: Jaina can use Sylvanas's name in her head easily now, but the tone stays tense. By chapter close she should sound less like she is lecturing downward.

team.label: partnership
team.operatingModeAtOpen: Two specialists sharing a road because neither can finish the job alone.
team.operationalChangeThisChapter: The village proves their work only holds when Jaina reads the arcane layer and Sylvanas reads the human one at the same time.
team.doNotOverstateRule: This is not trust or softness. It is the first time Jaina admits the other half of the method.

entries.rule: Still present with the riding party.
Still present with the riding party.
Not in this chapter. She has already departed north with the warning relay and the survivor girl.
Not in this chapter. She has already departed north under Neral's escort.

entries.character: Militia captain / village medic / granary keeper / cult initiate
entries.rule: Local one-chapter villagers for this outbreak site only.

castAndHandoffRules.chapterHandoffTarget: Chapter 5 opens with the same smaller riding party following the grain road southwest.

scenes.title: The Village That Is Not Dead Yet
scenes.sceneFunction: Show the plague while the village still thinks it is ordinary sickness, and split the work between Jaina's sickroom read and Sylvanas's people read.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The contamination is real; how it gets into people is still unknown.
scenes.arcPressure: Jaina can feel the wrongness first, but Sylvanas reads the human resistance faster; if they do not fuse those methods, the village will keep naming a weaponized outbreak as ordinary sickness.

enrichment.detail: Human border villages in Lordaeron should still feel fed and working on the surface: wet roads, grain economy, livestock, woodsmoke, and autumn labor. The horror here is ordinary routine persisting right up to collapse, not post-apocalyptic ruin.

Canon plague victims typically die within about three days and rise shortly after, so this chapter's 'ordinary sickness' stage should still feel deceptively plausible while coughs, sweetened breath, and mounting weakness are already inside the village.

beats.conflict: This settlement is still functioning, and that makes the danger harder to read because the locals see sickness, not plague.

outcome.summary: The party reaches a living site, but Jaina immediately feels the wrongness already threaded through ordinary routine.

beats.valueShift: investigative focus -> ordinary village with buried wrongness
newOnPageInformation: The same contamination can be observed before open collapse.
Most villagers still think they are dealing with an ugly run of sickness, not a spreading weapon.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How the sickness is getting in.
Whether intervention can do more than delay death.
sensoryAnchors: bread smoke
market noise cut with coughs
flushed child kept on her feet
sick-sweetness under ordinary village smells
beats.transitionIntent: access-pressure: the militia captain blocks easy access and forces Sylvanas to negotiate a workable role.
beats.verifierNotes: This living-village stage grows out of the same contamination thread already established on the south road. Do not present partial outbreak conditions as a brand-new category of threat.

beats.goal: Secure access without provoking a jurisdiction fight.
beats.conflict: The militia captain has no reason to trust armed elves in what still looks, to him, like a bad local sickness run.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas gets them inside by making Millhaven impossible to dismiss, but local trust stays thin and disbelief still slows everyone.

beats.valueShift: ordinary village with buried wrongness -> access under pressure
newOnPageInformation: Sylvanas can read exhausted authority and bargain with it instead of bulldozing it.
Jaina sees diplomacy where she expected force.
Jaina realizes Sylvanas is solving the room instead of dominating it, which is not what she expected from a soldier.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether what they learn here will generalize beyond this village.
sensoryAnchors: work-rough hands on the militia captain
Sylvanas lowering her voice instead of raising it
the weight of 'no one left' landing on the man
beats.transitionIntent: split-tasking: Jaina is sent to the infirmary while Sylvanas works the village through its doors, stores, and frightened people.
beats.verifierNotes: Jaina has already learned Sylvanas reads pressure, authority, and useful human weakness faster than expected. This beat deepens that correction; it must not reset Jaina to chapter1 contempt.

beats.goal: Map the contamination in living bodies.
beats.conflict: Jaina is looking at the same necromantic pattern she saw as residue in Millhaven, but now alive and spreading through tissue in real time.

outcome.summary: She can describe the stages in living bodies, but not yet how the contamination first gets in.

beats.valueShift: access under pressure -> whatever is doing this is getting into them somehow
newOnPageInformation: Early cases cluster contamination in the gut.
Only a handful of cases are sick enough to show the pattern spreading out from the gut, which means the body is taking it in and carrying it further.
The village medic has been treating it as fever because he has no better word for it.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the entry route is food, water, contact, or layered exposure.
sensoryAnchors: sweetness on infected breath
Jaina's magic on living tissue
borrowed cots in a room that was never meant to be an infirmary
beats.transitionIntent: pattern-pressure: Jaina doubles down on patient correlation while Sylvanas gathers the village's change history.
beats.verifierNotes: Jaina already knows the contamination is physical and active in living bodies. The new turn here is the gut-first clustering in current patients, not a surprise reveal that it can touch flesh before death.

scenes.title: Complementary Minds
scenes.sceneFunction: Let each woman solve half the trouble until the halves finally meet.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The contamination is getting into people somehow; the village's routines may hold the answer.
scenes.arcPressure: Jaina can isolate the magical carrier only if she lets Sylvanas's read of timing, labor, and supply stop feeling like secondary evidence.

enrichment.detail: Andorhal was Lordaeron's main agricultural distribution point and the plague spread through infected grain shipped from there to surrounding towns, which supports a village pattern of bread-heavy households worsening first while the source still sits farther down the road.
enrichment.source: Warcraft Wiki: Plague of Undeath; Warcraft Wiki: Andorhal

enrichment.detail: Andorhal should be understood less as a skyline than as loading yards, granaries, manifests, wagons, clerks, and grain dust everywhere — useful forward texture once the supply-chain answer clicks.

Jaina is canonically Antonidas's apprentice and one of the mages sent to investigate the plague, so her fast necromantic pattern-recognition and medical triage here should read as earned expertise rather than lucky guessing.

beats.conflict: Jaina can help some patients temporarily, but she cannot treat the underlying intake while the village keeps eating from the same stores.

outcome.summary: She proves partial intervention is possible, but only as delay, not cure.

beats.valueShift: whatever is doing this is getting into them somehow -> I can buy time, not victory
newOnPageInformation: Stabilization wards can slow progression in some cases.
Most patients are still early enough to help if the entry route is found quickly.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What the patients are all taking in that created the pattern.
sensoryAnchors: blue stabilization light over amber-wrong contamination
farmer's wife's hands going still
child watching Jaina work from the corner
beats.transitionIntent: correlation-pressure: Jaina now cross-references patient condition against diet and routine.
beats.verifierNotes: Treat the medical intervention as a new application of established plague-reading skill, not as the first proof that Jaina can act on the problem at all.

beats.conflict: Jaina has to sort the real clue from a pile of ordinary food and habit.

outcome.summary: She proves the contamination enters through ingestion and that heavy bread-eaters are the worst cases.

beats.valueShift: I can buy time, not victory -> the grain is the weapon
newOnPageInformation: Gut concentration is the real clue.
Bread consumption correlates with severity.
Home-grown oats or separate provisions correlate with lighter cases.
Repeated meals matter; this is cumulative settling in the body, not one bad swallow.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Where the tainted grain first joins the local stores.
sensoryAnchors: bakery in view from the infirmary door
grain dust on wood and clothes
Jaina gripping the frame when the pattern locks
beats.transitionIntent: supplier-pressure: Jaina now knows what is making people sick, but not who put it there.
beats.verifierNotes: This is the earned grain confirmation. Earlier Millhaven details remain precursor clues; do not retroactively write prior chapters as if grain had already been proved there.

beats.goal: Determine what changed in the village before the outbreak.
beats.conflict: The witnesses only remember routines, prices, deliveries, and practical changes — not magical theory.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas finds a supplier-and-timeline pattern that makes Millhaven's weak grain clue harder to dismiss, but they still cannot prove why it matters.

beats.valueShift: the grain is the weapon -> same supplier, same timeline
newOnPageInformation: A new grain supplier appeared three months back.
The same 'pleasant, forgettable, better prices' description surfaces again.
Sylvanas now has a supplier-and-road pattern that starts connecting this village to the weaker Millhaven clue.
Jaina can see from the window that Sylvanas is building a usable model of the village out of prices, deliveries, and fear.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether this supplier is cause or coincidence.
sensoryAnchors: grain-dusted hands of the keeper
Sylvanas asking very few questions and getting very specific answers
Jaina watching through the window while still half in medical triage
beats.transitionIntent: synthesis-pressure: the two partial pictures have to be laid beside each other.
beats.verifierNotes: Treat the supplier match as the first real strengthening of Millhaven's weak grain clue, not as retroactive certainty about the old trader or supplier identity.
beats.verifierModification: Clarified the outcome and one support bullet so the chapter strengthens the Millhaven clue without rewriting chapter3 into prior proof.

beats.dilemma: Both women now have partial explanations that fail alone.
beats.decision: Share immediately and act on the combined picture.
beats.valueShift: same supplier, same timeline -> tainted grain is being moved on purpose
newOnPageInformation: Jaina's answer and Sylvanas's answer fit exactly.
The granary is now a crime-scene target, not just a contaminated structure.
They are reading the deployment earlier than the villages themselves, but still after each delivery has already landed.
Jaina catches herself waiting for Sylvanas's half before trusting her own answer, which is new enough to be disorienting.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether they are dealing with a lone saboteur or an organization.
sensoryAnchors: grain dust on Sylvanas's gloves
residual ward-light on Jaina's hands
silence before both realize they reached the same picture from different directions
beats.transitionIntent: granary-rush: they go to the granary at once.
beats.verifierNotes: Operational convergence only. Jaina waiting for Sylvanas's half marks a method correction, not trust or softness.

scenes.title: The Granary
scenes.sceneFunction: Turn the sickness case into a hunt.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Grain is carrying the plague; the road and storehouses are spreading it.
scenes.arcPressure: Once the answers fuse, the granary has to prove the plague is a deliberate network with disposable people in it; otherwise Jaina can still pretend it is only a mechanism waiting to be solved.

enrichment.detail: Mal'Ganis commanded both the Scourge and the Cult of the Damned in Lordaeron while the plague network moved through infected grain and supervised handlers, which supports a low-level watcher as a frightened expendable cog rather than a mastermind.
enrichment.source: Warcraft Wiki: Mal'Ganis; Warcraft Wiki: Plague of Undeath

enrichment.detail: Andorhal fed northern Lordaeron through King's Road grain shipments, so 'follow the supply chain southwest' should feel like a concrete logistical trail of ledgers, wagons, granaries, and storehouses rather than a vague hunch.

beats.goal: Secure the grain stores and see how the grain is being doctored.
beats.conflict: A young initiate is inside actively monitoring the grain and reaches for a lamp to destroy the evidence.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas stops the fire and Jaina contains the broken agent, but his presence proves deliberate conspiracy.

beats.valueShift: tainted grain is being moved on purpose -> someone is guarding the operation
newOnPageInformation: The contamination exists in concentrated liquid form before mixing.
A live operative is stationed at village level to monitor the weapon.
sensoryAnchors: clay vessel shattering
sweet wrong smell of concentrated agent
Sylvanas's arrow pinning instead of killing
beats.transitionIntent: capture-pressure: interrogation becomes the next urgent priority.

beats.goal: Secure the prisoner and understand what the vessel means.
beats.conflict: The initiate is terrified, not disciplined-looking, and that makes him easy to underestimate while Jaina is forced to triage evidence fast.

outcome.summary: They hold him physically, and Jaina proves the liquid is skilled work, but they do not get usable words out of him.

beats.valueShift: someone is guarding the operation -> this is bigger than one frightened boy
newOnPageInformation: The alchemical design is far beyond hedge work.
The treatment sits on necromantic work Jaina knows only from warnings and half-whispered lessons about the things Dalaran does not teach openly.
The initiate is a cog, not a mastermind.
sensoryAnchors: torchlight on the prisoner's terrified face
barrier over the broken agent
Lyriel and Cyndia moving the prisoner under armed control
beats.transitionIntent: holding-cell pressure: the interrogation possibility collapses before it can be used.
beats.verifierNotes: Jaina already knows necromancy in the broad sense. The new information is the sophistication and deliberate refinement of this work, not the existence of necromancy itself.

beats.goal: Interrogate the initiate before the trail goes cold.
beats.conflict: By the time they reach the holding cell, the initiate has already killed himself with poison he was carrying for exactly this moment.

outcome.summary: The witness is dead, and his death proves somebody trained him for capture.

beats.valueShift: this is bigger than one frightened boy -> the village is tied to something larger and colder
newOnPageInformation: He was given a way to die before he could talk.
The plague operation has enough structure to treat village watchers as disposable hands.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: The exact name of the organization.
sensoryAnchors: warm body on the cell floor
bare trace of foam at the mouth
quiet horror in the room after they realize how quickly they lost him
beats.transitionIntent: supply-chain pressure: the only viable next move is to follow the supply chain southwest.

beats.dilemma: Stay and keep helping a village that still thinks it has a fever problem, or leave partial relief behind and go after the source before more places become Millhaven.
beats.decision: Follow the grain network toward Andorhal.
beats.valueShift: the village is tied to something larger and colder -> the trail has a destination
newOnPageInformation: The source is no longer just an idea; it has a road and a name: Andorhal.
Jaina and Sylvanas are now solving the same problem from opposite ends, and the picture only clears when they set their answers together.
sensoryAnchors: night coughing through otherwise ordinary houses
fading light from Jaina's hands after stabilization work
grain wagons imagined already moving somewhere southwest
beats.transitionIntent: chapter-hook: Chapter 5 opens on the smaller riding party following records, storehouses, and wagon routes toward Andorhal.
beats.verifierNotes: Carry forward cold collaborative method only. Chapter 5 inherits quicker mutual use, not easy warmth.

departuresLocked: The core riding party remains Jaina, Sylvanas, Cyndia, and Lyriel.
Militia captain, village medic, granary keeper, and the dead cult initiate are local to this outbreak site only.
factsLockedForChapter5: Grain is confirmed as what carries the plague into people.
The road and storehouse network is how it spreads.
A live village watcher existed and died by poison before he could be questioned.
The next target is the route southwest toward Andorhal.
Millhaven remains the first fully claimed village in the pattern; most other settlements are still only in confused early-stage sickness.
Millhaven left two weak grain-side anomalies in the file: the old seller on the north road and the survivor girl's memory of a newer grain man.
factsNotYetEarned: The Barov family connection as certainty.
Recognition of the old grain seller.
Any collapse of the partnership into open trust.

warnings: Beats 1-6 run six consecutive scene beats before the sequel at beat 7. Keep the infirmary and witness-pattern work sharply differentiated so the investigation keeps climbing instead of blending together.

Beats 8-10 stack three darkening scene beats in a row inside the granary sequence, with two no-and outcomes bracketing a yes-but. Modulate the pressure so each step changes the kind of danger, not just the amount.

Scene-beat outcomes lean heavily yes-but overall (six of the nine scene beats). The chapter has strong pressure, but the gains need different emotional weights so every discovery does not feel blunted in the same way.

chapterBridge: Intentional two-day carry from chapter3's southbound commitment: the same reduced four-rider party has pushed deeper into Lordaeron following stronger readings and still-living settlements, so chapter4 opens as the first on-page proof of the ordinary-looking outbreak the road ahead promised rather than as a reset.

craftResearch.findings: Keep the setting recognizably ordinary, then let small abnormalities poison it; suspense rises when bread smoke, labor, and market noise coexist with unmistakable wrongness.
craftResearch.source: Web craft survey: Jane Friedman, Dan Padavona, Authors Pathway

craftResearch.findings: Each discovery beat should come from action and should change the type of evidence, not merely repeat the same unease at higher volume.
craftResearch.source: Web craft survey: Authors Pathway, BookBaby, Novel Factory

craftResearch.findings: When two specialists share a chapter, each should own a distinct investigative lane and the chapter should turn when the two partial answers finally lock together.
craftResearch.source: Web craft survey: Novel Factory, Mystery Writer's Hub, Master of Worlds

craftResearch.findings: After a dense answer or revelation cluster, insert a short decision beat so the reader processes the implication before the next surge.
craftResearch.source: Web craft survey: Dan Padavona, StoryFlint, Jane Friedman

craftResearch.findings: Close with a fresh objective created by the new evidence, not with full solution; the answer should narrow the road ahead and sharpen pursuit.
craftResearch.source: Web craft survey: Novel Factory, StorytellingDB, Mystery Writer's Hub

structure: Beat 1 opens on active arrival and immediate assessment, which keeps the chapter from restarting with recap after chapter3's southbound hook.
Beats 1-3 escalate by changing evidence type from ambient wrongness to access conflict to body-mapping, so the opening investigation does not flatten into one note.
Beats 4-6 are a long analytical run with no sequel break, but they survive because each beat asks a different question: Can this be slowed? How does it enter? What changed in village routine?
Beat 7 lands at the right spot as the chapter's pressure-release and synthesis turn; without it, the granary sequence would feel like scene-pileup rather than pivot.
Beats 8-10 escalate the granary line from evidence preservation to technical diagnosis to proof of network discipline, and beat 11 converts that proof into a clean pursuit hook toward Andorhal.
character: The chapter stays inside Jaina's limited-third filter: every social read is phrased through what surprises, corrects, or irritates her.
Jaina remains active in beats 1, 3, 4, 5, 7, 8, 9, and 11; the beats where Sylvanas owns the lane are part of the forced-choice design, not passivity drift.
Sylvanas serves a distinct narrative function from Jaina throughout: she solves access, witness behavior, supply pattern, and tactical action instead of duplicating magical diagnosis.
Scene 1 and Scene 2 both earn their arcPressure claims because Jaina keeps reaching for the magical half first until Sylvanas's people-read becomes structurally indispensable.
The end-state shift is earned by method change rather than sentiment: Jaina leaves less superior and more collaborative, while the tone stays tense enough to avoid false trust.
continuity: Beat 1 correctly extends chapter3's established idea that ordinary settlements farther south can still hide the plague inside routine life.
Beat 3 advances the contamination thread instead of re-revealing Jaina's competence; the new information is gut-first clustering in living patients.
Beat 5 is the chapter that finally proves grain, so earlier Millhaven material must remain precursor clue rather than rewritten certainty.
Beat 6 originally leaned too close to retroactive proof; it has been clarified so the supplier match strengthens the Millhaven clue without pretending chapter3 already solved it.
Beat 9 needed a continuity warning because Jaina already knows necromancy exists; what is newly learned here is sophistication, secrecy, and scale.
transitions: Beat 1's access-pressure handoff lands cleanly in beat 2 because the militia captain becomes the immediate gate the chapter has to solve.
Beat 3's pattern-pressure transition carries into beat 4 without dead air: diagnosis moves directly into correlation and attempted mitigation.
Beat 6 and beat 7 form the chapter's best hinge, because synthesis-pressure resolves into a decision beat instead of another redundant clue pass.
Beat 9's holding-cell handoff works because the next beat turns physical custody into an immediate narrative loss, not a delayed off-page update.

The chapter opening uses an intentional cross-chapter break, but the new chapterBridge keeps the two-day skip oriented through the same reduced party, same southbound pursuit, and same outbreak logic.

pacing.notes: The chapter rises from ordinary unease (beats 1-2) to medical proof (3-5), shared synthesis (6-7), and conspiracy confirmation (8-10) before a brief pursuit-launch release in beat 11. The 9-scene / 2-sequel ratio is aggressive, but the evidence types change often enough to keep the middle from turning into investigative blur.

structure.notes: The opening is active, the midpoint turn lands when Jaina's medical answer and Sylvanas's supply answer lock together, and the close narrows the chase to Andorhal instead of resolving the problem. One continuity wording issue in beat 6 was softened so the chapter does not overstate what Millhaven had already proved.

character.notes: Jaina remains the POV and still drives the diagnostic half of the chapter, while Sylvanas, Cyndia, Lyriel, and the local witnesses all carry distinct functions. The chapter fulfills its arc promise by weakening Jaina's superiority without converting usefulness into warmth.

transitions.notes: All 11 beat-level transitionIntent chains were verified against the next beat or scene. No beat-level intents were missing, scene boundaries land cleanly, and the chapterBridge now frames the deliberate two-day chapter break as continuation rather than reset.

continuity.notes: 3 beats are new and 8 are evolutions; this is a forward-driving chapter with essentially no recap-only beats. One overclaim was corrected in beat 6, and anti-reintroduction guards were added for Jaina's plague competence, Sylvanas's human-read, the still-weak old-grain-seller clue, Jaina's necromancy knowledge, and the still-cold partnership dynamic.

modifications.description: Softened beat 6 so the supplier match strengthens Millhaven's weak grain clue without retroactively treating chapter3 as if it had already proved the supplier pattern.
